name: Tengen Uzui
subtitle: Flashy Sound Hashira presence
summary: Tengen carries a performance-driven charisma that makes every frame feel oversized, rhythmic, and impossible to ignore.
spotlight: Best used for loud compositions, metallic accents, and high-energy promotional art.

name: Kyojuro Rengoku
subtitle: Radiant flame-forward leadership
summary: Rengoku projects conviction and warmth at the same time, making him ideal for heroic key visuals and emotionally direct posters.
spotlight: Strong fit for warm palettes, crest motifs, and central hero layouts.

name: Obanai Iguro
subtitle: Serpentine restraint and sharp tension
summary: Obanai adds elegance, mystery, and controlled intensity, especially in compositions built around narrow framing and layered silhouettes.
spotlight: Works well in darker editorial art with thin line detail and contrast-heavy shadows.

name: Mitsuri Kanroji
subtitle: Heart-led power and bright emotion
summary: Mitsuri balances softness and explosive action, making her especially effective for expressive portraits and fan-centered merchandise art.
spotlight: Perfect for vibrant pink-green accents and emotionally open character marketing.

name: Muichiro Tokito
subtitle: Fog-drift calm with hidden force
summary: Muichiro brings an airy stillness that turns quiet scenes into something haunting, soft, and unexpectedly powerful.
spotlight: Great for washed gradients, drifting motion blur, and minimal atmospheric layouts.

name: Giyu Tomioka
subtitle: Still water discipline and solitude
summary: Giyu's reserved energy gives scenes weight and silence, creating a cooler visual counterbalance for louder personalities.
spotlight: Best for blue-gray palettes, reflective space, and understated dramatic framing.

name: Sanemi Shinazugawa
subtitle: Violent wind and scarred momentum
summary: Sanemi injects raw aggression and broken-edge intensity into the gallery, especially when compositions need volatility.
spotlight: Strong for jagged diagonals, distressed textures, and combat-heavy promotional sequences.

name: Gyomei Himejima
subtitle: Massive spiritual gravity
summary: Gyomei delivers scale, compassion, and solemn force, making him ideal for centered layouts that need emotional weight.
spotlight: Excellent for monumental framing, earth tones, and icon-like composition.

name: Tanjiro Kamado
subtitle: Empathy-driven protagonist energy
summary: Tanjiro anchors the gallery with warmth, resilience, and broad emotional accessibility, which makes him ideal for lead campaign visuals.
spotlight: Reliable centerpiece for key art, entry screens, and story-led homepage framing.

name: Kanao Tsuyuri
subtitle: Quiet observation with floral precision
summary: Kanao adds poise and delicacy, making her a strong choice for restrained portrait work and elegant feminine layouts.
spotlight: Good for petal motifs, quiet close-ups, and refined vertical compositions.

name: Yoriichi Tsugikuni
subtitle: Sun-born calm and overwhelming precision
summary: Yoriichi carries a sacred, untouchable level of swordsmanship that turns any composition into something mythic and absolute.
spotlight: Ideal for radiant blade trails, ancestral symbolism, and god-tier centerpiece illustrations.

name: Nezuko Kamado
subtitle: Soft innocence and explosive danger
summary: Nezuko is one of the strongest emotional anchors in the set, blending cuteness, volatility, and instant recognizability.
spotlight: Perfect for fan-favorite merchandise, emotional scene cards, and contrast-rich illustrations.

name: Zenitsu Agatsuma
subtitle: Fear and lightning in one frame
summary: Zenitsu creates dramatic contrast between nervous vulnerability and sudden electric brilliance, which is visually very effective.
spotlight: Excellent for bright yellow energy lines, comic tension, and sudden action beats.

name: Inosuke Hashibira
subtitle: Wild motion and feral speed
summary: Inosuke brings chaos, humor, and animal aggression, which makes him ideal for dynamic carousel moments and punchy motion graphics.
spotlight: Best for aggressive angles, shredded edges, and high-speed movement cues.

name: Shinobu Kocho
subtitle: Elegant poison and smiling danger
summary: Shinobu adds refined menace and grace, creating a beautiful but dangerous tone that reads instantly in portrait-led design.
spotlight: Strong for violet accents, graceful curves, and delicate-but-sharp visual storytelling.

name: Kanae Kocho
subtitle: Gentle grace with a dreamlike floral aura
summary: Kanae softens the gallery with warmth, serenity, and an almost weightless elegance that feels luminous without losing presence.
spotlight: Perfect for soft violet palettes, floral motion, and airy portrait-led storytelling.

name: Genya Shinazugawa
subtitle: Rough force and firearm-driven intensity
summary: Genya brings blunt aggression, grit, and a modern shock to the lineup, making him especially striking in darker action-heavy frames.
spotlight: Strong for red-black contrast, high-tension close-ups, and more violent promotional beats.
